content_en: BERKSHIRE HATHAWAY INC.
content_zh: 伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司

content_en: To the Shareholders of Berkshire Hathaway Inc.: This letter comes to you as part of Berkshire’s annual report. As a public company, we are required to periodically tell you many specific facts and figures. “Report,” however, implies a greater responsibility. In addition to the mandated data, we believe we owe you additional commentary about what you own and how we think. Our goal is to communicate with you in a manner that we would wish you to use if our positions were reversed – that is, if you were Berkshire’s CEO while I and my family were passive investors, trusting you with our savings.
content_zh: 致伯克希尔·哈撒韦公司各位股东：这封信是伯克希尔年度报告的一部分。作为上市公司，我们有义务定期向各位披露大量具体的事实和数据。然而，“报告”二字意味着更大的责任。除了法定披露的信息之外，我们认为还应当向各位补充说明你们持有的资产状况以及我们的思考方式。我们的目标是以换位思考的方式与各位沟通——也就是说，假设你是伯克希尔的CEO，而我和我的家人是被动投资者，将积蓄托付给你打理。

content_en: This approach leads us to an annual recitation of both good and bad developments at the many businesses you indirectly own through your Berkshire shares. When discussing problems at specific subsidiaries, we do, however, try to follow the advice Tom Murphy gave to me 60 years ago: “praise by name, criticize by category.” Mistakes – Yes, We Make Them at Berkshire Sometimes I’ve made mistakes in assessing the future economics of a business I’ve purchased for Berkshire – each a case of capital allocation gone wrong. That happens with both judgments about marketable equities – we view these as partial ownership of businesses – and the 100% acquisitions of companies.
content_zh:
这种方式促使我们每年坦诚汇报各位通过伯克希尔股票间接持有的众多企业的经营状况——好的坏的都讲。不过在讨论特定子公司的问题时，我们尽量遵循汤姆·墨菲60年前给我的忠告：“表扬点名，批评归类。”

错误——是的，伯克希尔也会犯错

有时候，我对伯克希尔所收购企业的未来经济前景判断失误——每一次都是资本配置出了差错。这种情况既发生在对上市股票的判断上——我们视其为企业的部分所有权——也发生在对公司的100%收购上。

content_en: At other times, I’ve made mistakes when assessing the abilities or fidelity of the managers Berkshire is hiring. The fidelity disappointments can hurt beyond their financial impact, a pain that can approach that of a failed marriage. A decent batting average in personnel decisions is all that can be hoped for. The cardinal sin is delaying the correction of mistakes or what Charlie Munger called “thumb-sucking.” Problems, he would tell me, cannot be wished away. They require action, however uncomfortable that may be.
content_zh: 还有些时候，我对伯克希尔聘用的管理者的能力或忠诚度判断失误。忠诚方面的失望所带来的伤害远超财务损失本身，那种痛苦几乎不亚于一段失败的婚姻。在用人决策上，能保持一个不错的“打击率”就已经是最好的期望了。最大的过错是拖延纠正错误——芒格称之为“吮拇指”。他常对我说，问题不会因为你视而不见就自行消失，必须采取行动，无论那有多么令人不适。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * During the 2019-23 period, I have used the words “mistake” or “error” 16 times in my letters to you. Many other huge companies have never used either word over that span. Amazon, I should acknowledge, made some brutally candid observations in its 2021 letter. Elsewhere, it has generally been happy talk and pictures. 1
content_zh: 在2019至2023年间，我在致各位的信中使用了16次“错误”这个词。许多其他大型公司在同一时期从未使用过这个词。我必须承认，亚马逊在其2021年的信中做出了一些极为坦率的反思。但除此之外，其他公司的股东信基本上都是报喜不报忧。

content_en: I have also been a director of large public companies at which “mistake” or “wrong” were forbidden words at board meetings or analyst calls. That taboo, implying managerial perfection, always made me nervous (though, at times, there could be legal issues that make limited discussion advisable. We live in a very litigious society.) * * * * * * * * * * * * At 94, it won’t be long before Greg Abel replaces me as CEO and will be writing the annual letters. Greg shares the Berkshire creed that a “report” is what a Berkshire CEO annually owes to owners. And he also understands that if you start fooling your shareholders, you will soon believe your own baloney and be fooling yourself as well. Pete Liegl – One of a Kind Let me pause to tell you the remarkable story of Pete Liegl, a man unknown to most Berkshire shareholders but one who contributed many billions to their aggregate wealth. Pete died in November, still working at 80.
content_zh:
我也曾担任大型上市公司的董事，在那些公司里，“错误”或“失误”是董事会会议和分析师电话会议上的禁忌词汇。这种暗示管理层永不犯错的禁忌总让我感到不安（虽然有时出于法律考虑，确实需要谨慎措辞。我们生活在一个极度好讼的社会里。）

到了94岁，格雷格·阿贝尔接替我出任CEO并撰写年度信件的日子已经不远了。格雷格认同伯克希尔的信条：年度“报告”是伯克希尔CEO每年欠股东的。他也深知，一旦你开始欺骗股东，很快你就会相信自己编的谎话，到头来连自己都骗了。

皮特·利格尔——独一无二的人

请允许我在此讲述皮特·利格尔的非凡故事。大多数伯克希尔股东并不认识他，但他为股东们创造了数十亿美元的财富。皮特于去年11月去世，享年80岁，直到生命最后一刻仍在工作。

content_en: I first heard of Forest River – the Indiana company Pete founded and managed – on June 21, 2005. On that day I received a letter from an intermediary detailing relevant data about the company, a recreational vehicle (“RV”) manufacturer. The writer said that Pete, the 100% owner of Forest River, specifically wanted to sell to Berkshire. He also told me the price that Pete expected to receive. I liked this no-nonsense approach. I did some checking with RV dealers, liked what I learned and arranged a June 28th meeting in Omaha. Pete brought along his wife, Sharon, and daughter, Lisa. When we met, Pete assured me that he wanted to keep running the business but would feel more comfortable if he could assure financial security for his family. Pete next mentioned that he owned some real estate that was leased to Forest River and had not been covered in the June 21 letter. Within a few minutes, we arrived at a price for those assets as I expressed no need for appraisal by Berkshire but would simply accept his valuation. Then we arrived at the other point that needed clarity. I asked Pete what his compensation should be, adding that whatever he said, I would accept. (This, I should add, is not an approach I recommend for general use.)
content_zh: 我第一次听说森林河公司——皮特在印第安纳州创立并管理的企业——是在2005年6月21日。那天我收到一位中间人的来信，详细介绍了这家休旅车（RV）制造商的相关数据。信中说，森林河的100%持有者皮特特别希望将公司卖给伯克希尔，并告知了他期望的价格。我喜欢这种直截了当的方式。我向一些房车经销商做了了解，对所得信息很满意，于是安排了6月28日在奥马哈的会面。皮特带来了他的妻子莎伦和女儿丽莎。见面时，皮特向我保证他想继续经营这家企业，但如果能确保家人的财务安全，他会更加安心。接着皮特提到他拥有一些租给森林河使用的房产，这部分在6月21日的信中没有涉及。几分钟之内，我们就达成了这些资产的价格——我表示伯克希尔不需要进行评估，直接接受他的估值。然后我们谈到了另一个需要明确的问题。我问皮特，他的薪酬应该是多少，并补充说无论他开什么价，我都接受。（我必须说明，这种做法我不建议普遍使用。）

content_en: Pete paused as his wife, daughter and I leaned forward. Then he surprised us: “Well, I looked at Berkshire’s proxy statement and I wouldn’t want to make more than my boss, so pay me $100,000 per year.” After I picked myself off the floor, Pete added: “But we will earn X (he named a number) this year, and I would like an annual bonus of 10% of any earnings above what the company is now delivering.” I replied: “OK Pete, but if Forest River makes any significant acquisitions we will make an appropriate adjustment for the additional capital thus employed.” I didn’t define “appropriate” or “significant,” but those vague terms never caused a problem. 2
content_zh: 皮特停顿了一下，他的妻子、女儿和我都向前探了探身。然后他的回答让我们大吃一惊：“嗯，我看了伯克希尔的委托书，我可不想比我的老板赚得多，所以每年付我10万美元就行了。”我差点从椅子上滑下来。皮特又补充道：“不过我们今年将赚到X（他说了一个数字），我希望对超出公司目前盈利水平的部分，每年能拿到10%的奖金。”我回答说：“好的，皮特，但如果森林河进行任何重大收购，我们会对因此投入的额外资本做出适当调整。”我没有定义“适当”或“重大”，但这些模糊的措辞从未造成任何问题。

content_en: The four of us then went to dinner at Omaha’s Happy Hollow Club and lived happily ever after. During the next 19 years, Pete shot the lights out. No competitor came close to his performance.
content_zh: 随后我们四人去了奥马哈的快乐山谷俱乐部共进晚餐，此后一切顺遂美满。在接下来的19年里，皮特的业绩令人叹为观止，没有任何竞争对手能望其项背。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * Every company doesn’t have an easy-to-understand business and there are very few owners or managers like Pete. And, of course, I expect to make my share of mistakes about the businesses Berkshire buys and sometimes err in evaluating the sort of person with whom I’m dealing.
content_zh: 并非每家公司都有一个简单易懂的业务模式，像皮特这样的老板或管理者更是凤毛麟角。当然，我预计自己仍然会在伯克希尔收购的企业上犯一些错误，有时也会看错合作对象的为人。

content_en: But I’ve also had many pleasant surprises in both the potential of the business as well as the ability and fidelity of the manager. And our experience is that a single winning decision can make a breathtaking difference over time. (Think GEICO as a business decision, Ajit Jain as a managerial decision and my luck in finding Charlie Munger as a one-of-a-kind partner, personal advisor and steadfast friend.) Mistakes fade away; winners can forever blossom. * * * * * * * * * * * * One further point in our CEO selections: I never look at where a candidate has gone to school. Never!
content_zh:
但我也收获了许多惊喜——无论是企业的潜力还是管理者的能力与忠诚度。我们的经验是，一个正确的决策，随着时间的推移可以带来惊人的回报。（想想GEICO作为一个商业决策，阿吉特·贾恩作为一个用人决策，以及我何其幸运地找到了查理·芒格这位独一无二的合伙人、私人顾问和忠实挚友。）错误会逐渐消退，但赢家可以永远绽放。

关于我们选择CEO还有一点：我从不看候选人上过什么学校。从来不看！

content_en: Of course, there are great managers who attended the most famous schools. But there are plenty such as Pete who may have benefitted by attending a less prestigious institution or even by not bothering to finish school. Look at my friend, Bill Gates, who decided that it was far more important to get underway in an exploding industry that would change the world than it was to stick around for a parchment that he could hang on the wall. (Read his new book, Source Code.) Not long ago, I met – by phone – Jessica Toonkel, whose step-grandfather, Ben Rosner, long ago ran a business for Charlie and me. Ben was a retailing genius and, in preparing for this report, I checked with Jessica to confirm Ben’s schooling, which I remembered as limited. Jessica’s reply: “Ben never went past 6th grade.” I was lucky enough to get an education at three fine universities. And I avidly believe in lifelong learning. I’ve observed, however, that a very large portion of business talent is innate with nature swamping nurture.
content_zh: 当然，有很多优秀的管理者毕业于最著名的学府。但也有很多像皮特这样的人，可能恰恰受益于就读一所不太显赫的学校，甚至根本没读完。看看我的朋友比尔·盖茨，他认为在一个将改变世界的爆发性行业中尽早起步，远比待在学校拿一张可以挂在墙上的文凭重要得多。（推荐阅读他的新书《源代码》。）不久前，我通过电话认识了杰西卡·图恩克尔，她的继祖父本·罗斯纳很久以前曾为查理和我经营一家企业。本是零售业的天才，在撰写本报告时，我向杰西卡确认了本的教育背景——我记得很有限。杰西卡的回答是：“本的学历没有超过六年级。”我有幸在三所优秀的大学接受了教育，我也热衷于终身学习。但我观察到，商业才能在很大程度上是与生俱来的，天赋远胜于后天培养。

content_en: Pete Liegl was a natural.
content_zh: 皮特·利格尔就是一位天生的商业奇才。

content_en: Last Year’s Performance In 2024, Berkshire did better than I expected though 53% of our 189 operating businesses reported a decline in earnings. We were aided by a predictable large gain in investment income as Treasury Bill yields improved and we substantially increased our holdings of these highly-liquid short-term securities. Our insurance business also delivered a major increase in earnings, led by the performance of GEICO. In five years, Todd Combs has reshaped GEICO in a major way, increasing efficiency and bringing underwriting practices up to date. GEICO was a long-held gem that needed major repolishing, and Todd has worked tirelessly in getting the job done. Though not yet complete, the 2024 improvement was spectacular. In general, property-casualty (“P/C”) insurance pricing strengthened during 2024, reflecting a major increase in damage from convective storms. Climate change may have been announcing its arrival. However, no “monster” event occurred during 2024. Someday, any day, a truly staggering insurance loss will occur – and there is no guarantee that there will be only one per annum.
content_zh:
去年的业绩

2024年，伯克希尔的表现超出了我的预期，尽管我们189家运营企业中有53%报告了盈利下降。我们受益于投资收入的大幅增长——这是可以预见的，因为国库券收益率提高了，而我们也大幅增加了这些高流动性短期证券的持仓。我们的保险业务同样实现了盈利大幅增长，其中GEICO的表现尤为突出。五年来，托德·库姆斯对GEICO进行了重大改造，提升了运营效率并使承保实践与时俱进。GEICO是一颗需要重新打磨的老牌宝石，托德不知疲倦地完成了这项工作。虽然尚未全部完成，但2024年的改善堪称惊艳。总体而言，2024年财产意外险的定价有所加强，反映出对流风暴造成的损害大幅增加。气候变化可能正在宣告它的到来。然而，2024年并未发生“巨灾”事件。但总有一天，一场真正令人震惊的保险损失将会发生——而且不能保证一年只发生一次。

content_en: The P/C business is so central to Berkshire that it warrants a further discussion that appears later in this letter.
content_zh: 财产意外险业务对伯克希尔如此核心，因此值得在本信后面做进一步讨论。

content_en: Berkshire’s railroad and utility operations, our two largest businesses outside of insurance, improved their aggregate earnings. Both, however, have much left to accomplish. Late in the year we increased our ownership of the utility operation from about 92% to 100% at a cost of roughly $3.9 billion, of which $2.9 billion was paid in cash with a balance in Berkshire “B” shares.
content_zh: 伯克希尔的铁路和公用事业运营——我们在保险业之外最大的两项业务——总体盈利有所改善。但两者都还有很多工作要做。去年底，我们将公用事业业务的持股比例从约92%提高到100%，总成本约为39亿美元，其中29亿美元以现金支付，余额以伯克希尔B类股支付。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * All told, we recorded operating earnings of $47.4 billion in 2024. We regularly – endlessly, some readers may groan – emphasize this measure rather than the GAAP-mandated earnings that are reported on page K-68.
content_zh: 总的来说，我们在2024年录得474亿美元的经营利润。我们一直——一些读者可能会抱怨说没完没了地——强调这一指标，而非K-68页上报告的GAAP法定利润。

content_en: Our measure excludes capital gains or losses on the stocks and bonds we own, whether realized or unrealized. Over time, we think it highly likely that gains will prevail – why else would we buy these securities? – though the year-by-year numbers will swing wildly and unpredictably. Our horizon for such commitments is almost always far longer than a single year. In many, our thinking involves decades. These long-termers are the purchases that sometimes make the cash register ring like church bells. 4
content_zh: 我们的衡量标准不包含我们持有的股票和债券的资本利得或损失，无论是已实现的还是未实现的。长期来看，我们认为收益很可能会占上风——否则我们为什么要买这些证券？——尽管逐年的数字会剧烈且不可预测地波动。我们对此类投资的视野几乎总是远超一年。在许多情况下，我们的思考以十年为单位。这些长期持有的投资，有时会让收银机像教堂的钟声一样响亮。

content_en: Here’s a breakdown of the 2023-24 earnings as we see them. All calculations are after depreciation, amortization and income tax. EBITDA, a flawed favorite of Wall Street, is not for us.
content_zh: 以下是我们对2023至2024年盈利的分项列示。所有数字均为扣除折旧、摊销和所得税后的金额。EBITDA——华尔街钟爱的有缺陷的指标——不适合我们。

content_en: (in $ millions)
content_zh: （单位：百万美元）

content_en: 2024 2023 Insurance-underwriting ..................... $ 9,020 $ 5,428 Insurance-investment income ................ 13,670 9,567 BNSF ................................... 5,031 5,087 Berkshire Hathaway Energy ................. 3,730 2,331 Other controlled businesses ................. 13,072 13,362 Non-controlled businesses* ................. 1,519 1,750 Other** ................................. 1,395 (175) Operating earnings ........................ $47,437 $37,350 * Includes certain businesses in which Berkshire had between a 20% and 50% ownership such as Kraft Heinz, Occidental Petroleum and Berkadia. ** Includes foreign currency exchange gains of approximately $1.1 billion in 2024 and approximately $211 million in 2023 produced by our usage of non-U.S. dollar-denominated debt.

Surprise, Surprise! An Important American Record is Smashed Sixty years ago, present management took control of Berkshire. That move was a mistake – my mistake – and one that plagued us for two decades. Charlie, I should emphasize, spotted my obvious error immediately: Though the price I paid for Berkshire looked cheap, its business – a large northern textile operation – was headed for extinction. The U.S. Treasury, of all places, had already received silent warnings of Berkshire’s destiny. In 1965, the company did not pay a dime of income tax, an embarrassment that had generally prevailed at the company for a decade. That sort of economic behavior may be understandable for glamorous startups, but it’s a blinking yellow light when it happens at a venerable pillar of American industry. Berkshire was headed for the ash can. Fast forward 60 years and imagine the surprise at the Treasury when that same company – still operating under the name of Berkshire Hathaway – paid far more in corporate income tax than the U.S. government had ever received from any company – even the American tech titans that commanded market values in the trillions. To be precise, Berkshire last year made four payments to the IRS that totaled $26.8 billion. That’s about 5% of what all of corporate America paid. (In addition, we paid sizable amounts for income taxes to foreign governments and to 44 states.) 5
content_zh:
惊不惊喜！一项重要的美国纪录被打破了

六十年前，现任管理层接手了伯克希尔。那是一个错误——我的错误——并且困扰了我们长达二十年。我必须强调，查理当时就立刻看出了我明显的失误：虽然我为伯克希尔支付的价格看起来很便宜，但它的业务——一家大型北方纺织企业——正走向消亡。美国财政部最先收到了伯克希尔命运的无声警告。1965年，这家公司没有缴纳一分钱的所得税，而这种尴尬状况在过去十年中一直如此。对于光鲜的初创企业，这种经济表现也许可以理解，但对一家老牌美国工业支柱来说，这是一个闪烁的黄色警示灯。伯克希尔正走向垃圾堆。

快进六十年，想象一下财政部的惊讶程度——同一家公司，仍然以伯克希尔·哈撒韦的名字运营，缴纳的企业所得税超过了美国政府从任何公司收到过的金额——甚至超过了那些市值数万亿美元的美国科技巨头。准确地说，伯克希尔去年向国税局缴纳了四笔合计268亿美元的税款，约占全美企业纳税总额的5%。（此外，我们还向外国政府和44个州缴纳了大量所得税。）

content_en: Note one crucial factor allowing this record-shattering payment: Berkshire shareholders during the same 1965-2024 period received only one cash dividend. On January 3, 1967, we disbursed our sole payment – $101,755 or 10¢ per A share. (I can’t remember why I suggested this action to Berkshire’s board of directors. Now it seems like a bad dream.) For sixty years, Berkshire shareholders endorsed continuous reinvestment and that enabled the company to build its taxable income. Cash income-tax payments to the U.S. Treasury, miniscule in the first decade, now aggregate more than $101 billion . . . and counting. * * * * * * * * * * * * Huge numbers can be hard to visualize. Let me recast the $26.8 billion that we paid last year.
content_zh:
请注意实现这一破纪录纳税额的一个关键因素：在1965至2024年的同一时期，伯克希尔股东只收到过一次现金股息。1967年1月3日，我们支付了唯一一次分红——总计101,755美元，即每股A类股10美分。（我已经记不清当初为什么向董事会提议这样做了。现在看来那简直像一场噩梦。）六十年来，伯克希尔的股东们支持持续再投资，这使得公司能够不断累积应税收入。向美国财政部缴纳的现金所得税，在第一个十年微不足道，如今累计已超过1010亿美元……而且还在增长。

巨大的数字可能难以形象化。让我换一种方式来呈现我们去年缴纳的268亿美元。

content_en: If Berkshire had sent the Treasury a $1 million check every 20 minutes throughout all of 2024 – visualize 366 days and nights because 2024 was a leap year – we still would have owed the federal government a significant sum at yearend. Indeed, it would be well into January before the Treasury would tell us that we could take a short breather, get some sleep, and prepare for our 2025 tax payments.
content_zh: 如果伯克希尔在整个2024年每20分钟向财政部寄出一张100万美元的支票——请想象一下，2024年是闰年，共有366个日日夜夜——到年底我们仍然欠联邦政府一大笔钱。事实上，要到1月份过了好一阵子，财政部才会告诉我们可以喘口气、睡个觉，然后准备缴纳2025年的税款。

content_en: Where Your Money Is Berkshire’s equity activity is ambidextrous. In one hand we own control of many businesses, holding at least 80% of the investee’s shares. Generally, we own 100%. These 189 subsidiaries have similarities to marketable common stocks but are far from identical. The collection is worth many hundreds of billions and includes a few rare gems, many good-but-far-from-fabulous businesses and some laggards that have been disappointments. We own nothing that is a major drag, but we have a number that I should not have purchased. In the other hand, we own a small percentage of a dozen or so very large and highly profitable businesses with household names such as Apple, American Express, Coca-Cola and Moody’s. Many of these companies earn very high returns on the net tangible equity required for their operations. At yearend, our partial-ownership holdings were valued at $272 billion. Understandably, really outstanding businesses are very seldom offered in their entirety, but small fractions of these gems can be purchased Monday through Friday on Wall Street and, very occasionally, they sell at bargain prices. We are impartial in our choice of equity vehicles, investing in either variety based upon where we can best deploy your (and my family’s) savings. Often, nothing looks compelling; very infrequently we find ourselves knee-deep in opportunities. Greg has vividly shown his ability to act at such times as did Charlie.
content_zh:
你的钱在哪里

伯克希尔的股权投资是“双手并用”的。一方面，我们控股许多企业，持有被投资公司至少80%的股份，通常是100%。这189家子公司与上市股票有相似之处，但远非相同。整个组合价值数千亿美元，其中包括几颗稀世珍宝、许多不错但远谈不上卓越的企业，以及一些令人失望的落后者。我们没有任何严重拖后腿的资产，但确实有一些我本不该买的。

另一方面，我们持有十几家家喻户晓的超大型高盈利企业的少量股份，如苹果、美国运通、可口可乐和穆迪。这些公司中许多在其运营所需的净有形资产上获得极高的回报率。年末，我们的部分持股市值为2720亿美元。

可以理解的是，真正杰出的企业极少被整体出售，但这些宝石的一小部分可以在华尔街每周一到周五买到，而且极偶尔会以低价出售。我们在选择投资工具时不偏不倚，根据哪里能最好地配置各位（以及我家人的）积蓄来做决定。大多数时候没有什么让人心动的机会；极少数时候我们会发现自己被机会淹没。格雷格已经生动地展示了他在这种时刻采取行动的能力，正如查理曾经做到的那样。

content_en: With marketable equities, it is easier to change course when I make a mistake. Berkshire’s present size, it should be underscored, diminishes this valuable option. We can’t come and go on a dime. Sometimes a year or more is required to establish or divest an investment. Additionally, with ownership of minority positions we can’t change management if that action is needed or control what is done with capital flows if we are unhappy with the decisions being made.
content_zh: 持有上市股票的好处是，当我犯错时更容易调转方向。但必须强调，伯克希尔目前的规模削弱了这一宝贵的选项。我们无法说进就进、说出就出。有时建仓或退出一项投资需要一年甚至更久。此外，作为少数股权持有者，即使有需要，我们也无法更换管理层，也无法控制资金流向——如果我们对正在做出的决策不满意的话。

content_en: With controlled companies, we can dictate these decisions, but we have far less flexibility in the disposition of mistakes. In reality, Berkshire almost never sells controlled businesses unless we face what we believe to be unending problems. An offset is that some business owners seek out Berkshire because of our steadfast behavior. Occasionally, that can be a decided plus for us.
content_zh: 对于控股企业，我们可以主导这些决策，但在处置错误投资方面的灵活性要小得多。现实中，伯克希尔几乎从不出售控股企业，除非我们面对的是自认为永无止境的问题。但有一点可以弥补：一些企业主正是因为我们这种坚定不移的风格而主动找上伯克希尔。有时候，这对我们来说是一个明显的优势。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * Despite what some commentators currently view as an extraordinary cash position at Berkshire, the great majority of your money remains in equities. That preference won’t change. While our ownership in marketable equities moved downward last year from $354 billion to $272 billion, the value of our non-quoted controlled equities increased somewhat and remains far greater than the value of the marketable portfolio. Berkshire shareholders can rest assured that we will forever deploy a substantial majority of their money in equities – mostly American equities although many of these will have international operations of significance. Berkshire will never prefer ownership of cash-equivalent assets over the ownership of good businesses, whether controlled or only partially owned. Paper money can see its value evaporate if fiscal folly prevails. In some countries, this reckless practice has become habitual, and, in our country’s short history, the U.S. has come close to the edge. Fixed-coupon bonds provide no protection against runaway currency. Businesses, as well as individuals with desired talents, however, will usually find a way to cope with monetary instability as long as their goods or services are desired by the country’s citizenry. So, too, with personal skills. Lacking such assets as athletic excellence, a wonderful voice, medical or legal skills or, for that matter, any special talents, I have had to rely on equities throughout my life. In effect, I have depended on the success of American businesses and I will continue to do so.
content_zh:
尽管一些评论人士认为伯克希尔目前持有非同寻常的巨额现金，但你们的绝大部分资金仍然投在股权资产中。这一偏好不会改变。虽然我们去年持有的上市股票从3540亿美元减少到2720亿美元，但非上市控股企业的价值有所增加，且仍然远超上市投资组合的价值。

伯克希尔的股东们可以放心，我们将永远把他们的大部分资金配置在股权资产中——主要是美国的股权资产，尽管其中许多企业有重要的国际业务。伯克希尔永远不会偏好持有现金等价物而放弃持有好企业，无论是控股还是部分持有。

如果财政愚行泛滥，纸币的价值可能会蒸发殆尽。在一些国家，这种鲁莽的做法已成惯例，而在我们国家短暂的历史中，美国也曾走到过悬崖边缘。固定票息债券无法抵御货币失控。然而，只要企业的产品或服务是国民所需要的，企业以及拥有特定才能的个人通常都能找到应对货币不稳定的办法。个人技能也是如此。

由于我不具备运动天赋、美妙嗓音、医学或法律专长——事实上没有任何特殊才能——我一生都不得不依靠股权投资。实际上，我一直依赖于美国企业的成功，未来也将继续如此。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * One way or another, the sensible – better yet imaginative – deployment of savings by citizens is required to propel an ever-growing societal output of desired goods and services. This system is called capitalism. It has its faults and abuses – in certain respects more egregious now than ever – but it also can work wonders unmatched by other economic systems. America is Exhibit A. Our country’s progress over its mere 235 years of existence could not have been imagined by even the most optimistic colonists in 1789, when the Constitution was adopted and the country’s energies were unleashed. 7
content_zh: 无论如何，公民的储蓄需要得到明智——最好是富有想象力——的配置，才能推动社会不断增长的商品和服务产出。这个体系叫做资本主义。它有缺陷和弊端——在某些方面比以往任何时候都更严重——但它也能创造其他经济体制无法匹敌的奇迹。美国就是最好的例证。我们国家在仅仅235年的历史中所取得的进步，是1789年宪法通过、国家能量得以释放时，即使最乐观的殖民者也无法想象的。

content_en: True, our country in its infancy sometimes borrowed abroad to supplement our own savings. But, concurrently, we needed many Americans to consistently save and then needed those savers or other Americans to wisely deploy the capital thus made available. If America had consumed all that it produced, the country would have been spinning its wheels. The American process has not always been pretty – our country has forever had many scoundrels and promoters who seek to take advantage of those who mistakenly trust them with their savings. But even with such malfeasance – which remains in full force today – and also much deployment of capital that eventually floundered because of brutal competition or disruptive innovation, the savings of Americans has delivered a quantity and quality of output beyond the dreams of any colonist.
content_zh:
诚然，我们的国家在建国初期有时需要从海外借款来补充自身的储蓄。但与此同时，我们需要许多美国人持续储蓄，然后需要这些储蓄者或其他美国人明智地配置由此产生的资本。如果美国消费了它生产的一切，这个国家只会原地打转。

美国的发展过程并不总是光鲜亮丽——我们的国家始终不乏骗子和推销者，他们试图利用那些错误地将积蓄托付给他们的人。但即便存在这种不法行为——它至今仍然猖獗——以及许多最终因残酷的竞争或颠覆性创新而失败的资本配置，美国人的储蓄所创造的产出在数量和质量上，都远超任何殖民者的梦想。

content_en: From a base of only four million people – and despite a brutal internal war early on, pitting one American against another – America changed the world in the blink of a celestial eye. * * * * * * * * * * * * In a very minor way, Berkshire shareholders have participated in the American miracle by foregoing dividends, thereby electing to reinvest rather than consume. Originally, this reinvestment was tiny, almost meaningless, but over time, it mushroomed, reflecting the mixture of a sustained culture of savings, combined with the magic of long-term compounding. Berkshire’s activities now impact all corners of our country. And we are not finished. Companies die for many reasons but, unlike the fate of humans, old age itself is not lethal. Berkshire today is far more youthful than it was in 1965. However, as Charlie and I have always acknowledged, Berkshire would not have achieved its results in any locale except America whereas America would have been every bit the success it has been if Berkshire had never existed. * * * * * * * * * * * * So thank you, Uncle Sam. Someday your nieces and nephews at Berkshire hope to send you even larger payments than we did in 2024. Spend it wisely. Take care of the many who, for no fault of their own, get the short straws in life. They deserve better. And never forget that we need you to maintain a stable currency and that result requires both wisdom and vigilance on your part.
content_zh:
从仅仅四百万人口起步——尽管早期经历了一场残酷的内战，美国人自相残杀——美国在弹指一挥间改变了世界。

伯克希尔的股东们以一种很小的方式参与了美国奇迹——放弃股息，选择再投资而非消费。最初，这种再投资微不足道、几乎毫无意义，但随着时间推移，它像蘑菇一样迅速膨胀，体现了持续储蓄文化与长期复利魔力的结合。伯克希尔的业务如今已遍及美国的每一个角落。而且我们还没有完成。企业会因各种原因消亡，但与人类的命运不同，衰老本身并不致命。今天的伯克希尔远比1965年时更加年轻。

然而，正如查理和我一直承认的，伯克希尔在任何其他地方都不可能取得今天的成就；而即使伯克希尔从未存在过，美国也一样会取得同样的成功。

所以，谢谢你，山姆大叔。总有一天，你在伯克希尔的侄子侄女们希望能向你缴纳比2024年更多的税款。请明智地花这些钱。照顾好那些并非因为自身过错而在人生中抽到下下签的人们，他们值得更好的待遇。永远不要忘记，我们需要你维持一个稳定的货币，而这需要你的智慧和警觉。

content_en: Property-Casualty Insurance P/C insurance continues to be Berkshire’s core business. The industry follows a financial model that is rare – very rare – among giant businesses. Customarily, companies incur costs for labor, materials, inventories, plant and equipment, etc. before – or concurrently with – the sale of their products or services. Consequently, their CEOs have a good fix on knowing the cost of their product before they sell it. If the selling price is less than its cost, managers soon learn they have a problem. Hemorrhaging cash is hard to ignore.
content_zh:
财产意外险

财产意外险（P/C）仍然是伯克希尔的核心业务。这个行业遵循一种在大型企业中极为罕见的商业模式。通常，企业在销售产品或服务之前或同时就会产生劳动力、材料、库存、厂房设备等成本。因此，CEO在卖出产品之前就能很好地掌握成本。如果售价低于成本，管理者很快就会发现问题所在。现金大量流出是很难被忽视的。

content_en: When writing P/C insurance, we receive payment upfront and much later learn what our product has cost us – sometimes a moment of truth that is delayed as much as 30 or more years. (We are still making substantial payments on asbestos exposures that occurred 50 or more years ago.)
content_zh: 而在承保财产意外险时，我们先收到保费，很久以后才知道我们的产品到底花了多少成本——有时这个真相时刻要延迟30年甚至更久。（我们至今仍在为50多年前发生的石棉赔偿案支付大笔赔款。）

content_en: This mode of operations has the desirable effect of giving P/C insurers cash before they incur most expenses but carries with it the risk that the company can be losing money – sometimes mountains of money – before the CEO and directors realize what is happening.
content_zh: 这种运营模式的好处是，财产意外险公司在承担大部分费用之前就能收到现金；但它也带来风险：公司可能正在亏钱——有时是巨亏——而CEO和董事们还浑然不知。

content_en: Certain lines of insurance minimize this mismatch, such as crop insurance or hail damage in which losses are quickly reported, evaluated and paid. Other lines, however, can lead to executive and shareholder bliss as the company is going broke. Think coverages such as medical malpractice or product liability. In “long-tail” lines, a P/C insurer may report large but fictitious profits to its owners and regulators for many years – even decades. The accounting can be particularly dangerous if the CEO is an optimist or a crook. These possibilities are not fanciful: History reveals a large number of each species. In recent decades, this “money-up-front, loss-payments-later” model has allowed Berkshire to invest large sums (“float”) while generally delivering what we believe to be a small underwriting profit. We make estimates for “surprises” and, so far, these estimates have been sufficient.
content_zh:
某些保险险种能将这种时间差降到最低，比如农作物保险或冰雹损害险，损失很快就能报告、评估和支付。但其他险种可能会让管理层和股东在公司走向破产的过程中仍然沉浸在喜悦之中。想想医疗事故险或产品责任险这类保障。在“长尾”险种中，一家财产意外险公司可能连续多年——甚至数十年——向股东和监管机构报告巨额但虚假的利润。如果CEO是一个乐观主义者或骗子，这种会计处理就会特别危险。这些可能性绝非空想：历史上两种人都大有人在。

近几十年来，这种“先收钱、后赔付”的模式使伯克希尔能够将大笔资金（“浮存金”）用于投资，同时我们相信总体上还实现了少许承保利润。我们会为“意外”预留准备金，到目前为止，这些预估都够用了。

content_en: We are not deterred by the dramatic and growing loss payments sustained by our activities. (As I write this, think wildfires.) It’s our job to price to absorb these and unemotionally take our lumps when surprises develop. It’s also our job to contest “runaway” verdicts, spurious litigation and outright fraudulent behavior. Under Ajit, our insurance operation has blossomed from an obscure Omaha-based company into a world leader, renowned for both its taste for risk and its Gibraltar-like financial strength. Moreover, Greg, our directors and I all have a very large investment in Berkshire in relation to any compensation we receive. We do not use options or other one-sided forms of compensation; if you lose money, so do we. This approach encourages caution but does not ensure foresight.
content_zh:
我们不会被日益增长的巨额赔付所吓退。（写下此文时，请想想野火。）我们的工作就是合理定价以吸收这些损失，并在意外发生时冷静承受。我们的工作还包括对抗“失控”的判决、无理的诉讼和赤裸裸的欺诈行为。

在阿吉特的领导下，我们的保险业务从一家默默无闻的奥马哈公司成长为世界领军者，以敢于承担风险和堪比直布罗陀的财务实力而闻名。此外，格雷格、我们的董事和我本人在伯克希尔的投资相对于我们获得的任何报酬来说都非常大。我们不使用期权或其他单方面的薪酬形式；如果你亏钱，我们也一样。这种方式鼓励谨慎，但并不能确保先见之明。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * P/C insurance growth is dependent on increased economic risk. No risk – no need for insurance.
content_zh: 财产意外险的增长取决于经济风险的增加。没有风险，就不需要保险。

content_en: Think back only 135 years when the world had no autos, trucks or airplanes. Now there are 300 million vehicles in the U.S. alone, a massive fleet causing huge damage daily. Property damage arising from hurricanes, tornadoes and wildfires is massive, growing and increasingly unpredictable in their patterns and eventual costs. 9
content_zh: 回想仅仅135年前，世界上还没有汽车、卡车或飞机。如今仅美国就有3亿辆机动车，这支庞大的车队每天都在造成巨大的损害。飓风、龙卷风和野火造成的财产损失规模巨大，且在不断增长，其模式和最终成本也越来越难以预测。

content_en: It would be foolish – make that madness – to write ten-year policies for these coverages, but we believe one-year assumption of such risks is generally manageable. If we change our minds, we will change the contracts we offer. During my lifetime, auto insurers have generally abandoned one-year policies and switched to the six-month variety. This change reduced float but allowed more intelligent underwriting. * * * * * * * * * * * * No private insurer has the willingness to take on the amount of risk that Berkshire can provide. At times, this advantage can be important. But we also need to shrink when prices are inadequate. We must never write inadequately-priced policies in order to stay in the game. That policy is corporate suicide.
content_zh:
为这些风险签发十年期保单将是愚蠢的——应该说是疯狂的——但我们认为一年期的风险承担通常是可控的。如果我们改变想法，就会改变提供的合同条款。在我的有生之年，汽车保险公司已经普遍放弃了一年期保单，转而采用六个月期。这一变化减少了浮存金，但使承保更加精明。

没有任何私营保险公司愿意承担伯克希尔所能提供的风险规模。有时候，这一优势至关重要。但当价格不合适时，我们也需要收缩业务。我们绝不能为了留在牌桌上而签发定价不足的保单。那种做法无异于企业自杀。

content_en: Properly pricing P/C insurance is part art, part science and is definitely not a business for optimists. Mike Goldberg, the Berkshire executive who recruited Ajit, said it best: “We want our underwriters to daily come to work nervous, but not paralyzed.” * * * * * * * * * * * * All things considered, we like the P/C insurance business. Berkshire can financially and psychologically handle extreme losses without blinking. We are also not dependent on reinsurers and that gives us a material and enduring cost advantage. Finally, we have outstanding managers (no optimists) and are particularly well-situated to utilize the substantial sums P/C insurance delivers for investment.
content_zh:
财产意外险的合理定价既是艺术也是科学，绝对不是乐观主义者该做的生意。招募阿吉特的伯克希尔高管迈克·戈德堡说得最好：“我们希望我们的承保人员每天上班时都感到紧张，但不至于瘫痪。”

综合考虑，我们喜欢财产意外险业务。伯克希尔在财务上和心理上都能毫不畏惧地承受极端损失。我们也不依赖再保险公司，这赋予了我们实质性的持久成本优势。最后，我们拥有出色的管理者（没有乐观主义者），并且特别适合利用财产意外险带来的大量资金进行投资。

content_en: Over the past two decades, our insurance business has generated $32 billion of after-tax profits from underwriting, about 3.3 cents per dollar of sales after income tax. Meanwhile, our float has grown from $46 billion to $171 billion. The float is likely to grow a bit over time and, with intelligent underwriting (and some luck), has a reasonable prospect of being costless. Berkshire Increases its Japanese Investments A small but important exception to our U.S.-based focus is our growing investment in Japan.
content_zh:
过去二十年来，我们的保险业务通过承保产生了320亿美元的税后利润，约为税后每美元销售收入3.3美分。与此同时，我们的浮存金从460亿美元增长到1710亿美元。浮存金可能会随时间继续小幅增长，并且在明智的承保（加上一些运气）下，有合理的前景实现零成本。

伯克希尔加大日本投资

我们以美国为重心的投资策略有一个虽小但重要的例外，那就是我们在日本不断增长的投资。

content_en: It’s been almost six years since Berkshire began purchasing shares in five Japanese companies that very successfully operate in a manner somewhat similar to Berkshire itself. The five are (alphabetically) ITOCHU, Marubeni, Mitsubishi, Mitsui and Sumitomo. Each of these large enterprises, in turn, owns interests in a vast array of businesses, many based in Japan but others that operate throughout the world. 10
content_zh: 伯克希尔开始购买五家日本公司股票已近六年，这五家公司以与伯克希尔颇为相似的方式非常成功地运营着。按字母顺序排列，它们分别是：伊藤忠商事、丸红、三菱商事、三井物产和住友商事。这些大型企业各自又持有众多企业的权益，许多总部在日本，其余则在全球各地开展业务。

content_en: Berkshire made its first purchases involving the five in July 2019. We simply looked at their financial records and were amazed at the low prices of their stocks. As the years have passed, our admiration for these companies has consistently grown. Greg has met many times with them, and I regularly follow their progress. Both of us like their capital deployment, their managements and their attitude in respect to their investors. Each of the five companies increase dividends when appropriate, they repurchase their shares when it is sensible to do so, and their top managers are far less aggressive in their compensation programs than their U.S. counterparts. Our holdings of the five are for the very long term, and we are committed to supporting their boards of directors. From the start, we also agreed to keep Berkshire’s holdings below 10% of each company’s shares. But, as we approached this limit, the five companies agreed to moderately relax the ceiling. Over time, you will likely see Berkshire’s ownership of all five increase somewhat.
content_zh:
伯克希尔于2019年7月首次买入这五家公司的股票。我们只是查阅了它们的财务记录，对其股票如此低的价格感到惊讶。随着时间推移，我们对这些公司的钦佩之情持续增长。格雷格与它们进行了多次会面，我也定期关注它们的进展。我们两人都欣赏它们的资本配置、管理团队，以及对待投资者的态度。五家公司都在适当时候提高股息，在合理时回购股票，而且其高管的薪酬方案远不如美国同行那样激进。

我们对这五家公司的持股是超长期的，我们承诺支持它们的董事会。从一开始，我们也同意将伯克希尔的持股保持在每家公司总股份的10%以下。但当我们接近这一上限时，五家公司同意适度放宽了这个限制。随着时间推移，各位可能会看到伯克希尔对这五家公司的持股比例都有所增加。

content_en: At yearend, Berkshire’s aggregate cost (in dollars) was $13.8 billion and the market value of our holdings totaled $23.5 billion.
content_zh: 截至年末，伯克希尔的总投资成本（以美元计）为138亿美元，持仓市值合计235亿美元。

content_en: Meanwhile, Berkshire has consistently – but not pursuant to any formula – increased its yen-denominated borrowings. All are at fixed rates, no “floaters.” Greg and I have no view on future foreign exchange rates and therefore seek a position approximating currency-neutrality. We are required, however, under GAAP rules to regularly recognize in our earnings a calculation of any gains or losses in the yen we have borrowed and, at yearend, had included $2.3 billion of after-tax gains due to dollar strength of which $850 million occurred in 2024. I expect that Greg and his eventual successors will be holding this Japanese position for many decades and that Berkshire will find other ways to work productively with the five companies in the future.
content_zh: 与此同时，伯克希尔持续——但并非按照任何公式——增加了以日元计价的借款。所有借款均为固定利率，没有浮动利率。格雷格和我对未来汇率没有任何预测，因此我们寻求接近货币中性的头寸。不过，根据GAAP准则的要求，我们需要定期在盈利中确认所借日元的汇兑损益，到年末我们已确认了因美元走强带来的23亿美元税后收益，其中8.5亿美元发生在2024年。我预计格雷格及其未来的继任者将持有这一日本投资组合数十年，伯克希尔也将在未来找到更多与这五家公司高效合作的方式。

content_en: We like the current math of our yen-balanced strategy as well. As I write this, the annual dividend income expected from the Japanese investments in 2025 will total about $812 million and the interest cost of our yen-denominated debt will be about $135 million. The Annual Gathering in Omaha I hope you will join us in Omaha on May 3rd. We are following a somewhat changed schedule this year, but the basics remain the same. Our goal is that you get many of your questions answered, that you connect with friends, and that you leave with a good impression of Omaha. The city looks forward to your visits. 11
content_zh:
我们也喜欢日元平衡策略目前的数学逻辑。在我撰写此文时，预计2025年日本投资的年度股息收入约为8.12亿美元，而我们日元计价债务的利息成本约为1.35亿美元。

奥马哈年度盛会

我希望各位能在5月3日加入我们在奥马哈的聚会。今年的日程安排有所调整，但基本内容不变。我们的目标是让各位得到许多问题的解答，与朋友们重逢，并带着对奥马哈的美好印象离开。这座城市期待着各位的到来。

content_en: We will have much the same group of volunteers to offer you a wide variety of Berkshire products that will lighten your wallet and brighten your day. As usual, we will be open on Friday from noon until 5 p.m. with lovable Squishmallows, underwear from Fruit of the Loom, Brooks running shoes and a host of other items to tempt you. Again, we will have only one book for sale. Last year we featured Poor Charlie’s Almanack and sold out – 5,000 copies disappeared before the close of business on Saturday. This year we will offer 60 Years of Berkshire Hathaway. In 2015, I asked Carrie Sova, who among her many duties managed much of the activity at the annual meeting, to try her hand at putting together a light-hearted history of Berkshire. I gave her full reign to use her imagination, and she quickly produced a book that blew me away with its ingenuity, contents and design.
content_zh: 我们将有同样出色的志愿者团队为各位提供种类繁多的伯克希尔产品，让各位钱包变轻、心情变好。照例，我们将于周五中午至下午5点开放，届时将有可爱的Squishmallows毛绒玩具、鲜果布衣的内衣、布鲁克斯跑鞋以及一系列诱人的商品。同样，我们今年只卖一本书。去年我们推出了《穷查理宝典》并售罄——5000册在周六营业结束前就被抢购一空。今年我们将推出《伯克希尔60年》。2015年，我请卡丽·索瓦——她在众多职责中负责管理年会的大部分活动——尝试编撰一本轻松有趣的伯克希尔历史。我给了她充分的自由发挥空间，她很快就制作出一本令我惊叹的书——创意、内容和设计都令人赞叹。

content_en: Subsequently, Carrie left Berkshire to raise a family and now has three children. But each summer, the Berkshire office force gets together to watch the Omaha Storm Chasers play baseball against a Triple A opponent. I ask a few alums to join us, and Carrie usually comes with her family. At this year’s event, I brazenly asked her if she would do a 60th Anniversary issue, featuring Charlie’s photos, quotes and stories that have seldom been made public. Even with three young children to manage, Carrie immediately said “yes.” Consequently, we will have 5,000 copies of the new book available for sale on Friday afternoon and from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. on Saturday.
content_zh: 后来，卡丽离开伯克希尔回归家庭，如今已有三个孩子。但每年夏天，伯克希尔的同事们都会聚在一起观看奥马哈追风者队与3A级对手的棒球比赛。我会邀请一些老同事参加，卡丽通常也会带着家人一起来。在今年的活动上，我厚着脸皮问她是否愿意制作一期60周年特刊，收录查理很少被公开的照片、语录和故事。即使要照顾三个年幼的孩子，卡丽还是立刻答应了。因此，我们将在周五下午和周六上午7点至下午4点提供5000册新书出售。

content_en: Carrie refused any payment for her extensive work on the new “Charlie” edition. I suggested she and I co-sign 20 copies to be given to any shareholder contributing $5,000 to the Stephen Center that serves homeless adults and children in South Omaha. The Kizer family, beginning with Bill Kizer, Sr., my long-time friend and Carrie’s grandfather, have for decades been assisting this worthy institution. Whatever is raised through the sale of the 20 autographed books, I will match.
content_zh: 卡丽拒绝为她在新版“查理”特刊上的大量工作接受任何报酬。我建议她和我共同签名20册，赠送给向斯蒂芬中心捐款5000美元的股东——该中心服务于南奥马哈的无家可归成人和儿童。基泽尔家族，从我的老朋友、卡丽的祖父老比尔·基泽尔开始，数十年来一直在资助这一值得支持的机构。通过20册签名书筹集的款项，我将一比一配捐。

content_en: * * * * * * * * * * * * Becky Quick will cover our somewhat re-engineered gathering on Saturday. Becky knows Berkshire like a book and always arranges interesting interviews with managers, investors, shareholders and an occasional celebrity. She and her CNBC crew do a great job of both transmitting our meetings worldwide and archiving much Berkshire-related material. Give our director, Steve Burke, credit for the archive idea. We will not have a movie this year but rather will convene a bit earlier at 8 a.m. I will make a few introductory remarks, and we will promptly get to the Q&A, alternating questions between Becky and the audience.
content_zh: 贝基·奎克将报道我们周六经过调整的年度聚会。贝基对伯克希尔了如指掌，总能安排与管理者、投资人、股东以及偶尔到场的名人的精彩访谈。她和CNBC团队在向全球直播我们的会议和存档大量伯克希尔相关资料方面做得非常出色。这个存档的创意要归功于我们的董事史蒂夫·伯克。今年我们不会放映电影，而是提前到上午8点开始。我将做几句开场白，然后直接进入问答环节，由贝基和现场观众轮流提问。

content_en: Greg and Ajit will join me in answering questions and we will take a half-hour break at 10:30 a.m. When we reconvene at 11:00 a.m., only Greg will join me on stage. This year we will disband at 1:00 p.m. but stay open for shopping in the exhibit area until 4:00 p.m. You can find the full details regarding weekend activities on page 16. Note particularly the always-popular Brooks run on Sunday morning. (I will be sleeping.) * * * * * * * * * * * * My wise and good-looking sister, Bertie, of whom I wrote last year, will be attending the meeting along with two of her daughters, both good-looking as well. Observers all agree that the genes producing this dazzling result flow down only the female side of the family. (Sob.) Bertie is now 91 and we talk regularly on Sundays using old-fashion telephones for communications. We cover the joys of old age and discuss such exciting topics as the relative merits of our canes. In my case, the utility is limited to the avoidance of falling flat on my face. But Bertie regularly one-ups me by asserting that she enjoys an additional benefit: When a woman uses a cane, she tells me, men quit “hitting” on her. Bertie’s explanation is that the male ego is such that little old ladies with canes simply aren’t an appropriate target. Presently, I have no data to counter her assertion.
content_zh:
格雷格和阿吉特将与我一起回答问题，我们将在上午10:30休息半小时。11:00重新开始后，只有格雷格会与我一起登台。今年我们将在下午1:00结束正式活动，但展览区将持续开放购物至下午4:00。各位可以在第16页找到周末活动的全部详情。特别注意周日早上一直很受欢迎的布鲁克斯跑步活动。（我将在睡觉。）

我去年写到过的聪慧且漂亮的姐姐伯蒂将出席本次会议，同来的还有她的两个女儿，也都很漂亮。观察者们一致认为，产生这种耀眼颜值的基因只沿着家族的女性一脉传递。（呜呜。）

伯蒂今年91岁了，我们每周日定期通话，用的是老式电话。我们畅谈年老的乐趣，讨论各种激动人心的话题，比如各自手杖的优劣。就我而言，手杖的用途仅限于防止摔个大马趴。但伯蒂总能胜我一筹，她声称自己还享受一个额外的好处：当一个女人拄着手杖时，男人就不会再来“撩”她了。伯蒂的解释是，男性的自尊心就是这样——拄着手杖的老太太根本不是合适的目标。目前，我没有数据来反驳她的说法。

content_en: But I have suspicions. At the meeting I can’t see much from the stage, and I would appreciate it if attendees would keep an eye on Bertie. Let me know if the cane is really doing its job. My bet is that she will be surrounded by males. For those of a certain age, the scene will bring back memories of Scarlett O’Hara and her horde of male admirers in Gone with the Wind. * * * * * * * * * * * * The Berkshire directors and I immensely enjoy having you come to Omaha, and I predict that you will have a good time and likely make some new friends. February 22, 2025 Warren E. Buffett Chairman of the Board 13
content_zh:
但我有所怀疑。在会议上我从台上看不太清楚，如果与会者能帮我留意一下伯蒂，我将不胜感激。请告诉我那根手杖是否真的在发挥作用。我打赌她身边会围满男性。对于一定年纪的人来说，这场景会让人想起《乱世佳人》中斯嘉丽·奥哈拉和她身边成群的男性崇拜者。

伯克希尔的董事们和我都非常高兴各位来到奥马哈，我预测各位将度过愉快的时光，并很可能结交一些新朋友。

2025年2月22日
沃伦·E·巴菲特
董事会主席
伯克希尔业绩 vs. 标普500（1965-1994）
